en.value:
Districts are the main implementers at the operational level. It supports the local communities in building their own bridges. As the beneficiaries themselves can best assess the need for a bridge, the communities initiate the bridge demand along with their commitment to mobilise local resources (labour and local materials), plus their willingness to assume responsibilities, prove the feasibility of the venture, and finally they construct a bridge. In this context, a comprehensive bridge demand of over 6000 bridges across the country has been compiled. Every year, according to the budget availability, these are screened and prioritised through a set of criteria such as distance gained, population served (giving higher weightage to DAGs), accidents occurred due to the absence of the bridge. The UC then takes the lead role for the completion of a bridge. For this purpose, an Agreement is established between the concerned UC and the DDC. Transparency is maintained at all stages through various tools like: public hearing/auditing, fact sheets, third party technical audit and participatory decision making process. Only local people are given the opportunity to work on the construction and the maintenance work of trail bridges. Another important aspect is the monitoring to ensure that equal wages are paid for equal work. Efforts are made to ensure that labourers are insured against death and accidents. Formation of an inclusive UC is one of the major tasks to ensure conflict sensitivity. The representation of women in the UCs is aimed at 33% with executive posts provisioned for DAGs in order to help address the needs of the DAG members.

	 

	Trail Bridge SWAp Coordination Committee ("TBSCC") is established as an apex level body comprising representatives of GoN as well as that of all development partners supporting TB SWAp, to give direction/guidance to the implementation of the TB SWAp. DoLIDAR is the executing agency on behalf of GoN and Trail Bridge Support Unit (TBSU)/Helvetas is the TA provider on behalf of SDC. SDC proposes to continue with Helvetas for this final phase (without going into a tendering process), because of their legacy and leading expertise in this field. Infact, SDC would not foresee a next phase without the involvement of Helvetas.
